scope.summary: Implement glTF 2.0 and VRM avatar static mesh import into tailor_avatars / tailor_body_proxies, reusing the gltf crate already present for animation import but targeting rest-pose geometry rather than animation tracks
acceptance_criteria: POST /tailor/avatars/import with format=gltf accepts a GLB or glTF+bin bundle and stores the default scene mesh in tailor_avatars with source_kind='gltf'

POST /tailor/avatars/import with format=vrm accepts a VRM file (GLB container with VRM extension) and stores with source_kind='vrm'; VRM human-bone map is extracted and stored in tailor_body_proxies for downstream capsule decomposition
Bone names extracted from glTF skin joints map to the standard skeleton schema used by the animation import path (R-ANIM-029 / MT-284) so a glTF avatar and its glTF animation load as a coherent pair
Emits TailorAvatarCreated on success; guard_authority_write enforced
Round-trip: imported glTF avatar feeds body proxy creation (MT-089) without additional format conversion
scope.risk_if_missed: glTF is the standard for VTuber and web-3D character delivery and VRM is the dominant VTuber avatar format. Without these import paths, Tailor is blind to the entire VTuber / virtual-character production market, blocking a growing segment of garment-design customers.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
